templates.description: A box-and-whisker plot showing the DISTRIBUTION of a measure: Circle marks at one point per grain member (the level dimension on the level-of-detail shelf), a continuous measure axis on rows ([sum:Measure:qk]), and a per-cell box-plot reference line (boxplot-whisker-type='standard') that draws the median / IQR box + whiskers over those points; an OPTIONAL facet dimension on cols splits the distribution into side-by-side boxes. Serves UC7 (distribution analysis) and the UC4 ranged/uncertainty view. Generalized from the repo distribution-bar-code-chart (a live-stamped strip plot: detail-grain dim on lod + aggregated measure axis) plus the distribution-box-whisker catalog exemplar's box-plot reference-line + Circle marks. Live render-verified 2026-07-06 by the golden-parity gate (composite 98; see portability_evidence.render_evidence): structural 0.97297 (5/5 critical), pixel 1.0, binding-sanity sane via reconciled 800=800=EXPECTED_COUNT Copy>Data mark counts (grain live-corrected from the disproven Segment-FD assumption) and caption-leak pass.
avoid_when: You want the frequency SHAPE across equal-width buckets (how many records fall in each range) — that is a histogram (distribution-histogram); a box plot summarizes to quartiles and hides the multi-modal shape a histogram reveals.

The grain (level) dimension is coarse — a box drawn over only a handful of points is statistical noise (quartiles/whiskers are unstable); bind a fine-grained entity (deal/ticket/order) as the level (see hazard sparse-extremes).
The ask is a single headline number or a trend over time — a box plot answers 'how is it spread / where are the outliers', not 'what is it now' or 'is it rising'.

slots.notes: The distributed measure — the continuous axis on rows, SUM([Measure]) per grain member → [sum:Measure:qk]. It is BOTH the axis-column and value-column of the box-plot reference line (the box + whiskers summarize this measure's values across the level members).

The GRAIN dimension on the level-of-detail (lod/detail) shelf — one Circle mark per member, and the population the box summarizes. REQUIRED because a box plot needs multiple marks per cell to have a distribution; an aggregated measure with no grain dim collapses to one point per cell (degenerate). Mirrors the bar-code chart's detail dim. XML column-instance [none:Level:nk].

OPTIONAL grouping dimension on cols — splits the distribution into side-by-side boxes (e.g. resolution-time distribution PER team). Because it is optional it is NOT counted in the required-bindable fixture_bind set (only {measure, level} are). When present it partitions the per-cell box plots; when absent the chart is a single box over the level members. XML column-instance [none:Facet:nk].

hazards.detail: A few extreme values stretch the measure axis so the IQR box compresses to a sliver and the median/quartile story is lost — the exact 'outliers swamp' condition the product use case calls out. The template applies no outlier handling (boxplot-mark-exclusion='false' keeps outlier marks in the axis scaling). Filter or exclude extreme points, or use a log axis, when a long tail dominates.

With few points per box (a coarse level dim, or a facet member with only a handful of grain members) the quartiles and whiskers are statistically meaningless — a box over 2–3 points is noise dressed as a summary. The template cannot know the per-cell count; bind a fine-grained level and confirm each box has enough members before reading the spread.

The box summarizes the measure ACROSS the level (grain) dimension's members. This is why 'level' is a REQUIRED slot even though the product sketch listed only {measure, optional facet}: with aggregation on and no grain dim, each cell has a single aggregated mark and the box is degenerate (one point). Bind the entity whose values you want distributed (deal/ticket/order) to 'level'; the facet is the optional grouping on top.

templates.description: A bullet/variance chart for actual-vs-target comparison: one Bar per category member (rep/region/segment on rows) whose length is the ACTUAL measure on cols ([sum:Actual:qk]), with a per-cell TARGET reference line (SUM([Target]) drawn as refline0) so the gap between the bar and the line reads as attainment/variance at a glance. Serves UC1 (sales performance & quota attainment). Generalized from the repo ranking-bullet-chart exemplar (bar + per-cell sum reference line); the Profit-Ratio color calc was dropped so the slot vocabulary is exactly {category, actual, target}. Structurally compiled hermetically, NOT live render-verified.
avoid_when: The ask is a running cumulative build-up to a total (revenue minus costs, contribution to a whole) — that is a waterfall; this bullet compares each member's actual against its own target independently, not a cumulative path.
There is no meaningful target/goal/benchmark measure to compare against — without the target reference line this collapses to a plain ranked bar (use ranking-ordered-bar).

The target is a rate/ratio or an already-period-total that is not additive at the category grain — the reference line SUMs the target per cell (formula='sum'), so a non-additive target double-counts (see hazard target-summed-per-cell).

slots.notes: The comparison dimension on rows — one bullet row per member (rep / region / segment). XML column-instance [none:Category:nk].
The ACTUAL/attained measure — the bar length on cols, SUM([Actual]) → [sum:Actual:qk]. This is also the reference line's axis-column (the target line is drawn on the same axis as the actual bar).

The TARGET/quota/goal measure. Carried on the level-of-detail (lod) shelf as SUM([Target]) → [sum:Target:qk] so it is in the view, and referenced by refline0 (value-column) as the per-cell target line. replaceFieldReferences rewrites [Target] in BOTH the lod instance and the reference-line on bind. See hazard target-summed-per-cell.

hazards.detail: A mid-period ACTUAL compared against a FULL-period target overstates the shortfall — the bar sits far below the target line simply because the quarter is not over, not because the member is behind. The template has no as-of/period-elapsed awareness: pro-rate the target to the elapsed period, scale the actual to a run-rate, or label the as-of date before reading the gap as risk.

A category member with no rows in the bound data produces NO bar (an inner-join / no-mark), so a zero-attainment rep/region silently disappears rather than showing a visible 0 bar beneath its target line — exactly the at-risk member the ask (“which reps are at risk of missing”) wants surfaced. Bind against data that carries every member (or densify/pad), or the chart hides its most important marks.

The target line is a per-cell reference line with formula='sum' (SUM([Target])), so it aggregates the target additively within each category cell. If the bound target is a rate/ratio, an average goal, or an already-rolled-up period total, SUM double-counts across the cell's rows and the line lands in the wrong place. Bind a target that is additive at the category grain, or switch the reference line's formula to average/median.

Product evidence (product team, 2026-07) - has bitten someone: when the actual and/or target rows carry a MIXED currency_code (USD + EUR + GBP ...), neither measure may be summed raw. SUM([Actual]) on cols and the per-cell SUM([Target]) reference line add different currency units as one number, so the attainment gap is silently WRONG (a data-correctness bug, not a formatting one). The template has no currency awareness. Convert to a single reporting currency, or filter to one currency_code, before binding a revenue/quota measure.

templates.description: An aggregated measure over an exact date with template-owned average and +/-3-sigma reference lines/bands; marks colored by an out-of-control (beyond +/-3-sigma) violation calc, not the raw measure.

slots.notes: XML instance is [md:Order Date:ok] derivation='MDY' (exact date, discrete/ordinal). 'md'/'MDY' is NOT a derivationMap key nor a §2.2 Derivation member. Normalized to the closest canonical token 'tdy' (TruncDay = day-level date). See non-canonical-template-derivation hazard for the fidelity caveat.

[sum:Profit:qk] drives rows (the individuals series) and the template-owned average / +/-3-sigma sample-stdev reference lines/bands. It NO LONGER drives color: color is the template violation calc (control_status_calc), so the same field is not on both a shelf and color and the redundant-color-encoding design gate is not tripped.

calcs.formula: IF ABS(SUM([Profit]) - WINDOW_AVG(SUM([Profit]))) > 3 * WINDOW_STDEV(SUM([Profit])) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
calcs.notes: XmR out-of-control flag: 1 when the point is beyond the +/-3-sigma control limits, else 0. WINDOW_AVG(SUM([Profit])) is the center line and 3*WINDOW_STDEV(SUM([Profit])) the band — the SAME limits the template's 'average' and sample-'stdev' (factor +/-3) reference lines draw, so color and bands agree. Referenced by [usr:Calculation_20260705120000:ok] (derivation='User' — an aggregate/table-calc field MUST use usr:, never none:, or the viz renders blank). Drives the color encoding (discrete: 1=red out-of-control, 0=blue in-control), replacing the former redundant [sum:Profit:qk] gradient.

hazards.detail: Template date instance uses derivation short-form 'md' (long 'MDY', an exact discrete date), absent from templates.ts derivationMap and the §2.2 Derivation union. Manifest records the closest canonical 'tdy' (TruncDay). RESIDUAL RISK: TruncDay (continuous day) differs from MDY (discrete exact date) at runtime; the fast-path date binding may render continuous rather than discrete. XML trusted for slot/field structure; derivation token normalized.

average and sample-stdev (+/-3 factor) reference lines/bands are bound to [sum:Profit:qk] and repaired only because Profit is a bound slot; no dataset-specific calc IDs are referenced, so this is not a DATASET_SPECIFIC_FORMULA blocker.

Color is driven by control_status_calc, a WINDOW_AVG/WINDOW_STDEV table calc (the +/-3-sigma beyond-limits flag). The column-instance carries no explicit <table-calc> Compute Using, so on live apply the window addressing defaults to the natural table partition (the Order Date series on cols). RESOLVED LIVE 2026-07-11 (oracle-leg composite 94): the default natural-table addressing computed on the applied render -- the User-derivation color instance and BOTH reference lines survived tableau-apply-worksheet mode=inline and reconciled 1242=1242 (see portability_evidence.render_evidence).

control_status_calc references the bare bindable [Profit]; templates.ts rewriteFormulaFieldRefs repairs it on bind because Profit is a bindable bare-key slot (same pattern as ww-ou-arrow). No template-internal calc-to-calc refs.

templates.description: A GENERIC over/under deviation arrow: one row per member, with a Line-mark shaft spanning the reference LINE value to the ACTUAL value on a shared measure axis (both measures folded via Measure Values, connected by a path on Measure Names) and a Shape-mark arrowhead at the actual end, colored by SIGN(SUM([Actual]) - SUM([Line])) so an over/actual-beats-line reads as one hue and an under as another. Three plain slots {member, actual, line} bound as ordinary categorical + two quantitative measures; the ONLY calc is the aggregate sign, which references the two bindable measure slots with plain arithmetic (SUM + subtraction + SIGN) and is rewritten on bind. Cloned from ww-ou-arrow's dual-mark (Line shaft + Shape arrowhead) arrow grammar but with EVERY compound-string-parse calc removed: no SPLIT/TRIM of a '<prefix> <winner>-<loser>' score string, no '<name> (<year>)' member-label parse, no season/id/roman-numeral text columns, and no inline O/U-diff spine. Intent keywords are deliberately DISTINCT from ww-ou-arrow's 'arrow-chart'/'over-under-arrow' chart-nouns (this template uses 'over-under' + the unique slug 'deviation-arrow'): those two nouns live in classify.ts CHART_NOUN_KEYWORDS, and a SECOND fast_path_eligible carrier of a chart-noun trips the lone-winner uniqueness contract (manifest.test.ts 'every classify.ts CHART_NOUN_KEYWORD is carried by <=1 fast_path_eligible manifest'), so carrying them would make this template un-stampable. Stamp-ready but deliberately UNSTAMPED (render_verified 'none' -> fast_path_eligible false) until the orchestrator live-verifies it.
avoid_when: The actual and/or reference values arrive as COMPOUND score/label strings that must be SPLIT-parsed (e.g. a 'KC 25-22' combined-score string, or a '<name> (<year>)' member label) - use ww-ou-arrow, whose calcs parse exactly those Super-Bowl-style shapes; this generic template takes two PLAIN numeric measures and does no string parsing, so a compound-string field binds the wrong value.

The framing is quota / attainment / actual-vs-goal (a rep or region measured against a quota or target as 'how much of goal attained'), where a bar with a per-cell reference line reads better - use quota-attainment-bullet; this arrow shows the directional over/under gap between two peer measures, not quota attainment.

A distinct over/under hue (e.g. teal over, red under) and left/right arrowhead GLYPHS are required on first render - that value->hue and value->glyph mapping is datasource-level style NOT carried by this worksheet-only template (see hazard datasource-style-dropped); the encodings' driving field is carried, but the marks fall back to the default palette + default shape until the style is re-injected at stamp time.

slots.notes: One arrow row per member on rows ([none:Member:nk]). The categorical the deviation is compared across (region / segment / rep).

The ACTUAL measure, SUM([Actual]) -> [sum:Actual:qk]. The far (arrowhead) endpoint of the shaft: it is the Shape pane's cols axis AND one of the two measures folded onto the shared Measure Values axis, AND the minuend of the sign calc. replaceFieldReferences rewrites [Actual] in the calc formula on bind.

The reference/target LINE measure, SUM([Line]) -> [sum:Line:qk]. The near endpoint of the shaft (the second measure folded onto the shared Measure Values axis via the Measure Names filter) and the subtrahend of the sign calc. replaceFieldReferences rewrites [Line] in the calc formula on bind. Superstore has no natural target column, so at stamp time bind a second real measure as the reference (e.g. Profit) or a genuine plan/target measure.

calcs.notes: SIGN(SUM([Actual])-SUM([Line])) -> +1 over / 0 tie / -1 under. Drives BOTH the arrow color and the arrowhead shape encoding. A generic, PLAIN-arithmetic aggregate calc (SUM + subtraction + SIGN) referencing only the two bindable measure slots - NO SPLIT/TRIM/parse; the compound-string-parse hazard of ww-ou-arrow is intentionally absent. Both bare refs rewrite on bind (raw-formula-refs). The discrete over/under hue + left/right glyph MAP is datasource-level style (see hazard datasource-style-dropped); this calc only supplies the driving field.

hazards.detail: Deviation family: the encoding reads as an OVER/UNDER direction, so bind a genuine actual measure and a genuine reference/target measure whose difference is meaningfully signed. If the reference is not a comparable peer measure (same unit, same grain), the over/under hue and the shaft length are not interpretable. Superstore has no built-in target column, so pick a real second measure (e.g. Profit as the reference) or a plan/target measure at stamp time.

The distinct over/under COLOR palette (e.g. teal over / red under) and the left/right arrowhead SHAPE glyph map are datasource-level <style> in the ww-ou-arrow golden, NOT expressible in a worksheet-only template. This template carries the color + shape ENCODINGS (which field drives them: the sign calc) but not the value->hue / value->glyph MAP, so on first apply the marks fall back to the default palette + default shape. Advisory only (surfaced via avoid_when), NOT a deterministic-path-blocking hazard; faithful hue/glyph is re-injected at stamp time.

The sign calc references the bare bindable [Actual]/[Line]; templates.ts rewriteFormulaFieldRefs repairs them on bind because each is a bindable bare-key slot (same pattern as deviation-diverging-bar / correlation-scatter-plot-chart). Gate #6 asserts both are bound. There are NO template-internal calc-to-calc refs and NO parse coercions (contrast ww-ou-arrow, whose INT(SPLIT(...)) chain is the compound-string-parse blocker this template removes).

templates.description: A diverging bar chart: one Automatic bar per category (Sub-Category on rows) whose length is a signed Profit-Ratio template calc (SUM([Profit])/SUM([Sales])) on cols, so bars extend left/right of zero and are colored on a diverging orange-blue ramp; sorted DESC by the ratio. Shows deviation above/below a zero baseline. Structurally compiled, not render-verified; the source Region='Central' filter rides along verbatim (HARDCODED_FILTER_MEMBERS).

slots.notes: One bar per member on rows; computed-sort orders these DESC by the Profit-Ratio calc.
Not on a shelf directly — feeds the Profit-Ratio template calc (numerator). replaceFieldReferences rewrites [Profit] inside the formula on bind.
Not on a shelf directly — feeds the Profit-Ratio template calc (denominator). replaceFieldReferences rewrites [Sales] inside the formula on bind.

calcs.notes: The signed diverging measure: SUM([Profit])/SUM([Sales]) drives the bar length (cols), the diverging color, AND the computed-sort. A self-closing <calculation>, so parseTemplateCalcs sees it — a declared calc slot; both bare refs rewrite on bind.

hazards.detail: Deviation family: the bar-length measure (Profit Ratio) can be NEGATIVE, so bars diverge left/right of a zero baseline and the color is a symmetric-around-zero diverging ramp. Bind a genuinely signed measure (a ratio, a variance, a delta) — a strictly-positive measure collapses to a one-sided ordinary bar and the diverging encoding wastes its palette.

The source workbook pins Region to a single INCLUSION member (function='member' Region='"Central"') kept on the Filters shelf (<slices>). This lane does NOT run the compile-time filter neutralizer (no XML edits), so the member rides along VERBATIM and is flagged HARDCODED_FILTER_MEMBERS: a bound dataset without a 'Central' region member renders empty. A follow-up can PARAMETERIZE it to unblock.

STRUCTURED hazard class read by the bind-time period guard (src/binder/period-guard.ts, W36-D-v1). The load-bearing filter here is a categorical MEMBER INCLUSION (Region='Central'), NOT a date-part period exclusion — so the incomplete-period guard deliberately DECLINES to veto this template on period grounds (it would be wrong to call a Region pin an 'incomplete period'). It stays ineligible via its HARDCODED_FILTER_MEMBERS blocker; its v2 path is member parameterization (collapse to level-members), not a period trim. Classified explicitly so the period guard's non-fire is auditable rather than incidental.

The Profit-Ratio calc references the bare bindable [Profit]/[Sales]; templates.ts rewriteFormulaFieldRefs repairs them on bind ONLY because both are bindable bare-key slots (same pattern as correlation-scatter-plot-chart). Gate #6 asserts both are bound.

templates.description: A gain/loss deviation chart: one custom Shape per member (Sub-Category on detail) placed on a signed y-axis where positive = rank-of-improvement and negative = rank-of-decline, over a Month/Year date on cols, colored by the period-over-period Sales difference. Every measure is a nested table-calc chain (difference-from-prior, if-positive/if-negative, rank, signed y-axis) that rides along verbatim. Structurally compiled, not render-verified.

slots.notes: The member grain on level-of-detail — one shape mark per member; also the addressing dimension the shelf table-calc instances partition/order by.
Month part of the SAME bound date field (inner cols axis). Reuses Order Date at a second derivation — see hazard single-date-two-derivations.
Year part of the SAME bound date field (outer cols axis, nested with Month) — also the ordering key of the difference/rank table calcs.

The base measure the gain/loss is computed from: ZN(SUM([Sales])) - LOOKUP(..., -1). It rides along inside the table-calc chain (not a declared calc slot); replaceFieldReferences rewrites [Sales] inside each formula on bind. Also on tooltip as [sum:Sales:qk].

hazards.detail: The one [Order Date] field is used at BOTH Month and Year on cols. Against the single-date committed fixture the second temporal slot cannot bind a DISTINCT field, so fixture_bind=false — a valid, honest state (same as change-over-time-calendar-heatmap). On a real dataset both slots bind the same date field via qualified keys.

Every measure is a table calc: Calculation_44402728363008000 (ZN(SUM([Sales])) - LOOKUP(..., -1), the diff-from-prior), if-negative / if-positive splits, RANK_UNIQUE of each, and the signed y-axis. Each <calculation> nests a <table-calc>, so it is NON-self-closing and parseTemplateCalcs (self-closing only) does NOT see it — NONE are declared calc slots; they ride along verbatim (same class as ranking-bump-chart table-calc-rank-rides-along). The bindable bare refs ([Sales]) rewrite on bind; the calc-to-calc references (Calculation_X -> Calculation_Y) are template-internal and stable.

The table-calc ordering/compute-using addresses reference source fields by name — <order field='[Category]'> and ordering-field='[Category]' inside the difference/rank calcs. [Category] is NOT a declared bindable slot (it is not on any shelf), so replaceFieldReferences does not rewrite it: the addressing rides along referencing a literal 'Category' the bound dataset may not carry, which can reset the table-calc partition/ordering. The shelf instances re-address by [Sub-Category]+[yr:Order Date:ok], so the primary grain is covered, but the vestigial [Category] ordering is a residual portability risk.

The Shape mark uses a workbook-local custom shape image (<format attr='shape' value='Custom/noun_585136.png'/>). That asset is not part of the template/worksheet fragment and is absent on apply, so marks fall back to the default shape — cosmetic fidelity loss, not a binding break.

Deviation family: the y-axis is a SIGNED rank (positive = rank-of-improvement, negative = -rank-of-decline), so members split above/below a zero baseline (declining <- | -> improving). The read depends on the difference being meaningfully signed across the bound members; a strictly-positive series collapses the diverging axis.

templates.description: A spine chart: one row per category (Nationality) split into two contrasting components that diverge left/right of a center line (e.g. Women | Men), each component a signed share-of-total template calc so the pair reads as a two-sided deviation. RED: the source worksheet has NO {{DATASOURCE}} placeholder (bound to a federated datasource id) and its component calcs bake dataset-specific member literals ([Gender]='Men'/'Women') — not injectable as-is.

slots.notes: The comparison dimension on rows — one spine row per member. The only genuinely bindable structural field; the two component measures are template-owned baked calcs.

calcs.notes: The right-side (positive) component: share of total contributed by the 'Men' member. BAKES the member literal [Gender]='Men' and references bare [Value]/[Gender] as template-INTERNAL (not declared slots), so it cannot be generically bound — the dataset must literally carry Gender/Value with a 'Men' member (see hazard baked-member-literals / blocker DATASET_SPECIFIC_FORMULA).
A constant nominal center-divider label ('Women | Men'). Template-owned decorative dimension; carries no bindable inputs.

The left-side (NEGATIVE) component: share of total contributed by the 'Women' member, NEGATED so it extends left of the center line — the signed measure that makes this a deviation spine. Same baked-literal non-portability as component_positive.

hazards.detail: The worksheet has NO {{DATASOURCE}} placeholder: <datasources> and every field reference are bound to the literal federated datasource id 'federated.1ko0q9z0cybhx212227b819gayqi'. The inject/apply path fills {{DATASOURCE}} to retarget a template onto a new datasource — with none present the template cannot be retargeted and would carry the source datasource id verbatim. Flagged NO_DATASOURCE_PLACEHOLDER; a follow-up must re-parameterize the datasource id to make this template injectable.

The two component measures bake dataset-specific member literals into their formulas — SUM(if [Gender]='Men' ...) and -SUM(if [Gender]='Women' ...) — and reference bare [Value]/[Gender] that are NOT declared bindable slots (template-internal inputs). replaceFieldReferences cannot rewrite a string literal member ('Men'/'Women') and does not touch template-internal refs, so the calcs only compute on a dataset that literally carries Gender/Value with those members. Flagged DATASET_SPECIFIC_FORMULA (fail-closed): a portable spine would parameterize the split dimension + its two member values.

Deviation family: the 'Women' component is a NEGATED share (-SUM(...)/SUM([Value])) so the two components diverge left/right of a zero/center line — the defining signed-measure structure of a spine chart. The read requires exactly two complementary components whose signed shares oppose across the divider.

Three categorical filters pin source members verbatim: Measure='"Weighted Sample"', a Measure-Names filter enumerating the two component measures, and the spine-label filter member='"Women     |     Men"'. These are source-specific pins kept on the Filters shelf (<slices>); on any other dataset they match nothing. Not separately blocked because the NO_DATASOURCE_PLACEHOLDER + DATASET_SPECIFIC_FORMULA blockers already gate this template far below fast-path.

cols is [Multiple Values] (Measure Values) carrying the two component calcs, and color is [:Measure Names] — Tableau pseudo fields generated by the two-measure layout, not bindable fields, so they ride along and need no slot.

detail/grain dimension carrying semantic-role='[State].[Name]'; cosmetic hint carried onto the target.

templates.description: A histogram: one CONTINUOUS measure is binned into equal-width buckets on the X axis (cols) and the COUNT of records per bucket sets the bar heights on the Y axis (rows). The bin is a template-owned calc column ([Profit (bin)], class='bin') derived from the same measure that is counted. Factory-compiled hermetically from the repo distribution-histogram exemplar (Superstore Profit); live render-verified 2026-07-06 by the golden-parity gate (composite 95; see portability_evidence.render_evidence) BUT ELIGIBILITY WITHHELD (fast_path_eligible=false): the DATASET_SPECIFIC_FORMULA blocker (the bin width is dataset-derived — the leg adapted 283→500 by hand-rewrite) stands until a bind-time bin-width adapter computes it per dataset, and a render proof does not clear that blocker.
avoid_when: The field is categorical / discrete — a histogram bins a CONTINUOUS measure; for counts per named category use a bar chart, not a histogram.
The ask is to compare a metric across named categories or over time — a histogram shows the distribution SHAPE of one measure, not cross-category magnitude or a trend.

The bound measure's range differs greatly from the source measure's — the template's hardcoded bin width (size=283) does not auto-recompute on bind and must be reviewed, or the viz over/under-bins (one giant bar or thousands of slivers).

slots.notes: The single bindable measure. It is COUNTed on rows (Y axis) as [cnt:Profit:qk] AND is the input the bin calc [Profit (bin)] buckets on cols — the classic Tableau histogram default (one field, binned + counted). Binds to any measure; base column [Profit], shelf instance [cnt:Profit:qk].

calcs.notes: The DEFINING distribution slot: a template-owned bin column (<calculation class='bin' size='283' peg='0' decimals='2' formula='[Profit]'/>) placed on cols as [none:Profit (bin):qk] (with a show-full-range). It is treated as a first-class calc — its formula ref [Profit] is rewritten to the bound measure on apply (forcing the 'measure' slot to bind) — but its bin WIDTH (size) is a dataset-derived parameter that does NOT recompute on bind (see the bin-size-dataset-specific hazard / DATASET_SPECIFIC_FORMULA blocker).

hazards.detail: The bin column carries size='283' / peg='0' baked from the source measure's (Profit's) value range. On bind the bin's formula ref [Profit] is rewritten to the bound measure, but the bin WIDTH attribute is NOT recomputed — a measure with a much smaller range collapses to one bar, a much larger range explodes into thousands of slivers. The bin width is the histogram's defining parameter, so this is a load-bearing, dataset-specific formula parameter (fast_path_blocker DATASET_SPECIFIC_FORMULA) that a human/data-aware step must retune after binding, not a cosmetic note. PRODUCT EVIDENCE (UC7 distribution analysis): bin width is STORY-CHANGING, not merely cosmetic — too few bins hide the shape, too many shatter it into noise, and the right count is dataset-dependent, so the retune is an analytical decision the author must own. Separately, OUTLIERS can SWAMP the chart: a long tail forces a wide domain so the bulk of the distribution crushes into the first bin or two; exclude/cap extremes or use a log axis when a few extreme values dominate the range. The baked size=283 is authored-dataset-specific — derived from the source measure's (Superstore Profit's) value range this template was compiled from — so it is a starting point to retune per dataset, never a portable constant (product team, 2026-07).

Product evidence (product team, 2026-07) — has bitten someone: under ~30 rows a histogram's shape is statistical NOISE, not a distribution. A handful of records scattered across equal-width bins reads as a random staircase and invites over-interpretation of bumps that would vanish on the next data pull. Below roughly 30 observations prefer a BAR CHART of the individual values (or a dot/strip plot); a histogram earns its shape only once each bin holds enough observations to be stable.

The bin column ([Profit (bin)], kind=calc, bindable=false) and its instance [none:Profit (bin):qk] are template-owned and are NOT rewritten on bind — only the bin's formula ref [Profit] is. So after binding to e.g. Sales the X-axis caption still reads 'Profit (bin)'. Cosmetic (the viz renders correctly); relabel the bin field/axis on the bound sheet if the source-measure name must not leak.

ELIGIBILITY IS WITHHELD NONETHELESS (fast_path_eligible=false): the DATASET_SPECIFIC_FORMULA blocker (the bin width is dataset-derived — the leg adapted 283→500 via rewrite) is NOT cleared by a render proof; it stands until a bind-time bin-width adapter computes the width per dataset. See portability_evidence.render_evidence for the auditable basis.

templates.description: A funnel/conversion chart for staged pipeline drop-off: an ORDERED stage dimension on rows ([none:Stage:nk], data/ordinal order preserved — deliberately NO computed sort) and the amount measure as a Bar on cols ([sum:Amount:qk]), also on the size encoding so the bars taper toward the narrow end of the funnel. Reading top-to-bottom the shrinking bars show stage-to-stage conversion / drop-off. Serves UC6 (pipeline health & deal flow). Tableau has no native funnel mark, so this is authored as creative Bar usage generalized from the proven ranking-ordered-bar structure (NO usable funnel exemplar exists in the twb corpus — grep 'funnel' returns nothing). Live render-verified 2026-07-06 by the golden-parity gate (composite 98; see portability_evidence.render_evidence): structural 0.97297 (5/5 critical), pixel 1.0, binding-sanity sane via reconciled 4=4 Copy>Data mark counts and the positive no-captions caption-leak pass.
avoid_when: The stages have no inherent sequence and you want members ranked by value — use ranking-ordered-bar (which sorts DESC by the measure); a funnel encodes a fixed conversion ORDER, not a value ranking.
You need proportional multi-path flow between many source/target nodes — that is a sankey, not a single-path funnel.

The amount is not monotonically non-increasing across the stages (a later stage can carry more than an earlier one) — the funnel silhouette assumes drop-off and inverts when a stage rises (see hazard stage-skipping).

slots.notes: The ORDERED pipeline-stage dimension on rows — one funnel band per stage. The template applies NO computed sort, so stages render in the bound field's member order; bind an ordinal/ordered stage field (or set a manual member sort) so the conversion sequence is correct. See hazard stage-order-dependence. XML column-instance [none:Stage:nk].

The stage amount (deal value / count) — SUM([Amount]) → [sum:Amount:qk], the SAME instance on cols (bar length) AND on the size encoding (bar taper). One derivation, so no qualified key. replaceFieldReferences rewrites [Amount] in both places on bind.

hazards.detail: The template plots stages in the BOUND dimension's native member order (there is deliberately NO computed-sort element — sorting DESC by amount would reorder stages and destroy the sequence). A funnel's whole story is the fixed stage order (Lead → Qualified → Proposal → Closed); binding an unordered nominal stage field yields an arbitrary, meaningless funnel. Bind an ordinal/ordered stage field or set a manual member sort before trusting the silhouette.

Deals that skip stages, or are counted at more than one stage, break the monotonic drop-off the funnel assumes: a later stage can exceed an earlier one, so the silhouette inverts and the implied conversion rates mislead. The template cannot detect non-monotonic stages — validate the data is a clean single-count-per-stage funnel (or model conversion explicitly) before reading drop-off from the bars.

Tableau has no native funnel mark; this is an ordered Bar with amount on both length and size (taper) — a left-aligned conversion bar, NOT a centered/symmetric funnel. A true mirrored funnel needs a dual-axis with a negated companion measure, which the {stage, amount} slot vocabulary does not express. The chart communicates stage drop-off honestly but is not a geometrically symmetric funnel; do not claim symmetry.

Product evidence (product team, 2026-07) - has bitten someone: a pipeline amount summed across rows with a MIXED currency_code (USD + EUR + GBP ...) adds different currency units as one number, so each stage bar and the implied conversion are silently WRONG. SUM([Amount]) feeds BOTH the bar length (cols) and the taper (size) with no currency awareness. Convert to a single reporting currency, or filter to one currency_code, before binding a monetary pipeline measure.

Compiled HERMETICALLY via the factory route (no live Tableau Desktop) and SUBSEQUENTLY live render-verified on 2026-07-06: portability_evidence.render_verified='live-2026-07-06' and fast_path_eligible=true. The golden-parity gate earned the stamp (composite 98: structural 0.97297, pixel 1.0, binding-sanity sane via reconciled 4=4 Copy>Data mark counts + the positive no-captions caption-leak pass) — the live render-readback proof the hermetic compile alone could not supply. See portability_evidence.render_evidence for the auditable basis.

templates.description: A Gantt chart: one GanttBar per row-hierarchy member (Category > Sub-Category > Product Name > Order ID) laid out over a Year/Month date on cols, its length sized by a duration calc (Ship Lag = DATEDIFF of two dates) and colored by a status calc (Ship Lag Indicator) — a schedule/duration view over time. Lane W11-F2: the three source filter pins (product-name list, sub-category, year) were PARAMETERIZED (collapsed to level-members) so those fields stay data-driven Filters pills with no source literals.

slots.notes: Outermost row grouping of the schedule hierarchy.
Second row grouping, nested under Category.
Third row grouping, nested under Sub-Category.
Leaf row grain — one Gantt bar per member (the individual task).
Year part of the SAME bound start-date field (outer cols axis). Reuses Order Date at a second derivation — see hazard single-date-two-derivations.
Month part of the SAME bound start-date field (inner cols axis).

The END date. Not on a shelf; it feeds the Ship Lag duration calc (DATEDIFF of Order Date -> Ship Date). A DISTINCT second date field is required — with the single-date fixture only one date exists, so fixture_bind=false (see hazard single-date-two-derivations).

calcs.notes: Ship Lag — the duration (bar length/size) between the start (Order Date) and end (Ship Date). Requires both bindable date slots.

calcs.formula:
If [Calculation_291608117815463936] >= 5 then "Late"
ElseIf [Calculation_291608117815463936] >=2 Then "On-Time"
Else "Early"
END
calcs.notes: Ship Lag Indicator — buckets the duration into Late/On-Time/Early for the bar color. Template-internal: its only input is the Ship Lag calc, not a bindable slot.

hazards.detail: The source workbook pinned Product Name to a nine-member product list. Lane F2's compile-time filter policy PARAMETERIZED it: the member union was collapsed to the neutral all-members form (<groupfilter function='level-members' level='[none:Product Name:nk]'/>), so Product Name stays a data-driven Filters-shelf pill (its <slices> entry preserved) with the source literals removed.

templates.description: A task-rollup Gantt: exactly ONE GanttBar per task (categorical on rows), positioned by MIN(start_date) on a CONTINUOUS green date axis on cols (each task's EARLIEST start), sized by a summed duration measure (bar length) and colored by a phase dimension. The MIN aggregation is what makes the grain one-bar-per-task: an aggregated continuous date does not partition, so — when phase is functionally dependent on task — the render collapses to COUNTD(task) marks (offline-pinnable count 17 on the bundled Sample - Superstore when task=Sub-Category, phase=Category). Distinct from gantt-timeline-chart, whose cols is a per-date Day-Trunc [tdy:Start Date:qk] grain (a data-dependent bar count, one bar per date), and from gantt-chart, whose schedule MATRIX uses discrete Year/Month parts + two DATEDIFF calcs. PROVENANCE: this is the one-bar-per-task grain the INDEPENDENT gantt oracle (evals/runs/lane-i-smoke/make-gantt-oracle-twb.mjs -> ~/TableauGoldens/lab/gantt-oracle.twb) validly stamps — MIN(start) aggregated + task-grain rollup, EXPECTED_COUNT 17 on Superstore; W26-C identified the grain mismatch that made gantt-timeline-chart's per-date variant unstampable by that oracle. Factory-route template, generalized to placeholders + generic field names; live render-verified 2026-07-06 by the golden-parity gate (composite 98; see portability_evidence.render_evidence): structural 0.97297 (5/5 critical), pixel 1.0, binding-sanity sane via reconciled 17=17=EXPECTED_COUNT Copy>Data mark counts and caption-leak pass.
avoid_when: The task has intra-task structure that matters — multiple segments, gaps, or per-date activity within one task. MIN(start_date) rolls every task up to a SINGLE bar from its earliest start, HIDING those gaps; for one bar per activity date use the per-date gantt-timeline-chart variant instead.

The dataset has no numeric duration/length measure to size the bars — a GanttBar with no size collapses to zero-length ticks; bind a real duration (days/periods) measure to the size slot or choose a dot/strip timeline.

The ask needs a schedule MATRIX broken out by discrete Year/Month columns with a computed ship/lead lag and Late/On-Time/Early status across a start AND an end date — that is gantt-chart; this rollup plots one continuous MIN(start) axis and a plain duration measure.

slots.notes: The task/activity label on rows — exactly one GanttBar row per member (the rollup grain). XML column-instance [none:Task:nk].

The bar's START position on the CONTINUOUS date axis, AGGREGATED as MIN (green [min:Start Date:qk]) = each task's EARLIEST start. The MIN aggregation (not a per-date Day-Trunc) is what collapses the grain to one bar per task — an aggregated continuous date does not partition. A single date field at a single derivation, so no qualified key is required. See hazards min-start-rollup-hides-intra-task-gaps + continuous-min-start-axis-required.

The bar LENGTH (task duration in days/periods) on the size encoding — SUM([Duration]) -> [sum:Duration:qk]. A plain length measure, NOT a DATEDIFF calc, so the template binds a single-date schema. Must bind a REAL duration/length measure — see hazard gantt-size-real-duration-measure.

The phase/status dimension on color — one hue per member. XML column-instance [none:Phase:nk]. REQUIRED (not optional): the stampable oracle semantics carry color=phase in the default render, and an optional color slot would have to sit OFF-shelf (dropping color) or dangle an unbound pill — so the vocabulary forces required here. When phase is functionally dependent on task the rollup grain stays one-bar-per-task.

The defining rollup: cols carries [min:Start Date:qk], an AGGREGATED continuous MIN date (derivation Min), so an aggregated date does not partition and the grain collapses to exactly one GanttBar per task at its EARLIEST start. This deliberately HIDES intra-task gaps, multiple segments, and any per-date structure within a task — the honest cost of the rollup versus gantt-timeline-chart's per-date [tdy:Start Date:qk] grain. If per-date/segment detail matters, use the timeline variant, not this rollup.

The GanttBar length is driven by SUM([Duration]), a plain quantitative measure, not a DATEDIFF(end,start) duration calc (contrast gantt-chart's Ship Lag). This is what lets the template bind a single-date schema, but the size slot MUST bind a REAL duration/length-in-days measure — binding an unrelated measure sizes the bars by that measure instead of task duration, and binding nothing collapses the bars to zero-length ticks.

cols carries a CONTINUOUS MIN-aggregated date [min:Start Date:qk] (green pill) — the rollup start axis. The start_date slot must bind a real date/datetime field; binding a non-date field (or a discrete date part) collapses the axis to categorical headers and the GanttBars lose their time positions.

hazards.detail: The source workbook filters Year of Order Date with an EXCLUSION (function='except') that removes {2024, 2025} — almost certainly a partial/incomplete-recent-year guard (a paired-bar magnitude read would be distorted by short partial-year bars). The factory filter policy fails CLOSED on exclusions: dropping or neutralizing an exclusion WIDENS the data (re-includes the removed years) in a potentially result-corrupting direction, and the excluded years are source-calendar literals. It is kept VERBATIM and flagged HARDCODED_FILTER_MEMBERS — the template is not fast-path eligible until a follow-up re-expresses the exclusion as a declared 'exclude incomplete periods' slot. (The apply-path 3c neutralizer would collapse it to all-members if Year is remapped, silently defeating the guard — the blocker documents that this must be a conscious decision, not an accident.)

STRUCTURED hazard class read by the bind-time period guard (src/binder/period-guard.ts, W36-D-v1). The Year-of-Order-Date EXCLUSION above is a load-bearing incomplete-latest-period guard at the YEAR grain. At bind time only field metadata is visible (no row data), so the guard CANNOT prove the bound data's latest year is complete, nor that the hardcoded excluded members {2024, 2025} name the actual incomplete period. When a date is bound it therefore VETOES the fast path (escalate 'incomplete-period-unattested') unless the caller attests period-completeness (periodCompletenessAttested). The HARDCODED_FILTER_MEMBERS blocker and the hardcoded-filter-blocking prose hazard REMAIN — v1 documents the path and does not flip fast_path_eligible; v2 will compute a data-driven trim.

templates.description: Horizontal bars, one per category; bar length = the measure's ABSOLUTE magnitude (a size comparison across categories). Bars are sorted descending by the measure purely as a READING AID — the sort is not a ranking contract (no top-N, best/worst, or positional semantics). The magnitude family's generic {category, measure} bar: cloned from ranking-ordered-bar's two-slot categorical+quantitative bar shape, but with ranking-specific keywords, subtitle, and sort-role labels removed, and NO period/temporal slot or hardcoded incomplete-period filter (the HARDCODED_FILTER_MEMBERS blocker that keeps magnitude-paired-bar / magnitude-paired-column-chart off the fast path). Live-stamped 2026-07-11 (oracle-leg composite 95, 3=3 reconciled, computed-sort survived apply): fast_path_eligible true -- the magnitude family's first fast-path template.
avoid_when: The user explicitly wants a ranked or top-N / bottom-N ordering as the point of the chart (best/worst, leaders/laggards) - reach for ranking-ordered-bar or ranking-ordered-column, where the sort order IS the contract; here the descending sort is only a reading aid.
The comparison is period-over-period (this year vs last year, before vs after) - use the magnitude paired-bar or paired-column family, which pairs a measure over two or more time periods per group.

slots.notes: One horizontal bar per member on rows. Also the dimension instance the computed-sort orders (DESC by the measure) - a reading aid, not a ranking contract.

Bar length on cols = the measure's magnitude, aggregated as SUM. Template_field 'Sales' is just the placeholder column the field-swap injector renames to the bound measure; the computed-sort's using= references this instance.

hazards.detail: The computed-sort binds the category dimension instance ([none:Category:nk]) to the measure instance ([sum:Sales:qk]); both slots must bind or the sort dangles. Both are required, so this is satisfied by coverage. (This is the ONLY carry-over from the ranking donor's sort mechanic; unlike ranking, the sort here is presentational, not a top-N contract.)

templates.description: A Pareto chart: Sales bars per category (Sub-Category on cols, sorted DESC by Sales) layered with a cumulative-percent-of-total line (a compound CumTotal+PctTotal table calc), plus two 80% reference lines driven by a Parameter to spotlight the 'vital few'. A Measure-Names combo (bar + line) on rows. Structurally compiled, not render-verified; the 80% threshold parameter lives in a separate Parameters datasource (PARAMETER_REQUIRED).

slots.notes: The ranked category on cols — one bar per member; computed-sort orders these DESC by SUM(Sales) so the cumulative line climbs from the largest contributors.

The measure: SUM([Sales]) is the bar height AND the base of the cumulative-percent line ([pcto:cum:sum:Sales:qk]). Used at a single base derivation (Sum) in both, so no qualified key is required; also the computed-sort key.

calcs.notes: The 80% Pareto threshold. In the XML it is a <column><calculation> (a constant 0.8) inside a SEPARATE <datasource name='Parameters'>, so parseTemplateCalcs classifies it as a calc slot — but it is really a PARAMETER: both reference lines take value-column='[Parameters].[Parameter 3]'. Non-bindable and template-owned; the Parameters datasource + this parameter must exist on apply (see hazard parameter-threshold-required / blocker PARAMETER_REQUIRED).

hazards.detail: The cumulative line rides on [pcto:cum:sum:Sales:qk], a <column-instance> of base [Sales] carrying TWO nested <table-calc> elements composed together — CumTotal (running sum) THEN PctTotal (percent of total) — i.e. a compound running-percent-of-total. Because it is a column-INSTANCE with nested table-calcs (not a <column><calculation>), parseTemplateCalcs (self-closing only) never sees it: it is NOT a declared calc slot and rides along verbatim. Its base is the bindable 'sales' slot; replaceFieldReferences rewrites [Sales] inside the instance on bind. The compound order (cumulate, then normalize) is load-bearing — both table-calc addressings must survive the bind for the Pareto curve to be correct.

The two 80% reference lines take value-column='[Parameters].[Parameter 3]' — a parameter in a SEPARATE <datasource name='Parameters'> declared alongside {{DATASOURCE}}. That datasource + parameter are NOT covered by the {{DATASOURCE}} placeholder, so on apply the Parameters datasource and Parameter 3 must already exist or the reference lines dangle. Flagged PARAMETER_REQUIRED (fail-closed): a follow-up can re-express the threshold as a template-owned constant or a declared parameter slot to unblock.

rows is a Measure-Values expression ([sum:Sales:qk] + [pcto:cum:sum:Sales:qk]) rendered across three panes — Bar (Sales), Bar, and Line (cumulative %) — colored by [:Measure Names]. The bar/line combo IS the Pareto shape; Measure Names is a Tableau pseudo field generated by the two-measure rows expression, not a bindable field, so it rides along and needs no slot.

avoid_when: More than ~3-5 categories - humans compare angles poorly; a sorted horizontal bar reads more precisely and scales to any count

templates.description: A proportional (100%) stacked bar: one full-width bar per category (Region on rows) whose axis is percent-of-total Sales (a PctTotal table calc on cols), segmented by a second dimension (Category on color) and with mark size driven by a FIXED [Region] LOD of SUM([Sales]) so each region's absolute magnitude reads through its bar thickness. Shows each category's composition normalized to 100%. Structurally compiled, not render-verified.

slots.notes: The category on rows — one proportional bar per member; also the FIXED-LOD grain ({ FIXED [Region]: SUM([Sales]) }). Template column carries aggregate-role-from='[State/Province]' (cosmetic secondary-role hint). replaceFieldReferences rewrites [Region] on the shelf AND inside the LOD formula on bind.

The measure on cols as a percent-of-total ([pcto:sum:Sales:qk:3], a PctTotal table calc that rides along) AND the FIXED-LOD numerator. Used at a single base derivation (Sum) in both, so no qualified key is required.
The stacking dimension on color — the segments composing each region's 100% bar.

calcs.notes: The 'Regional Sales' FIXED LOD sizing each region's bar by its absolute total ({ FIXED [Region]: SUM([Sales]) }). A self-closing <calculation>, so parseTemplateCalcs sees it — a declared calc slot. Both refs are bindable slots (region, sales); replaceFieldReferences rewrites them on bind. The LOD grain assumes the bound row dimension IS the aggregation grain (see hazard fixed-lod-grain).

hazards.detail: The size measure is a FIXED [Region] LOD ({ FIXED [Region]: SUM([Sales]) }). replaceFieldReferences rewrites [Region] to the bound row dimension, so the LOD grain follows the binding — but the design ASSUMES the bound row dimension is the correct aggregation grain. Binding a row dimension finer/coarser than the intended magnitude grain makes the FIXED value (and thus the bar thickness) mismatch the visible bar's grain.

The cols axis [pcto:sum:Sales:qk:3] is a <column-instance> of base [Sales] carrying a nested <table-calc type='PctTotal'> (Percent of Total). Because it is a column-INSTANCE with a nested table-calc (not a <column><calculation>), parseTemplateCalcs (self-closing <calculation> only) never sees it — it is NOT a declared calc slot and rides along verbatim on cols. Its base is the bindable 'sales' slot; replaceFieldReferences rewrites [Sales] inside the instance on bind. The PctTotal normalizes each bar to 100% across the addressed partition.

Region carries a <manual-sort> whose <dictionary> lists source members ('South','Central','East','West'). The buckets are source-calendar literals; on a differently-membered bound dimension they do not match and the manual sort silently resets to default order (cosmetic — no binding break, no data change).
The FIXED LOD references the bare bindable [Region]/[Sales]; templates.ts rewriteFormulaFieldRefs repairs them on bind ONLY because both are bindable bare-key slots. Gate #6 asserts both are bound.

templates.description: A waterfall (bridge) chart: GanttBar floating bars whose tops sit on a running total ([cum:sum:Profit:qk] on rows) and whose lengths are the negative per-category measure (-SUM([Profit]) on size), so each category shows its incremental contribution to a cumulative whole across an ordered categorical axis (Sub-Category on cols, sorted DESC by SUM(Profit)). Color is a continuous SUM(Profit) encoding. Factory-compiled from the CookBook 'Waterfall Viz' worksheet; structurally compiled and render-verified (live-2026-07-06).
avoid_when: The ask is about a smooth trend or trajectory over continuous time — a line/area chart shows the running trajectory better than a waterfall's discrete running-total bars.

The bound categorical has no meaningful order and you do not sort it by the measure — the running total is order-dependent, so an arbitrary member order yields an arbitrary cumulative path (this template sorts Sub-Category DESC by SUM(Profit); binding an unordered dimension without that sort breaks the bridge).
You only want to compare per-category magnitudes (not their cumulative build-up) — a ranked bar chart compares values more directly than a running-total waterfall.

slots.notes: The measure whose per-category contributions build the waterfall. Bound as SUM on color ([sum:Profit:qk]); the SAME base [Profit] also feeds the running-total [cum:sum:Profit:qk] on rows (a CumTotal table calc that rides along — hazard running-total-table-calc-rides-along) and the -SUM([Profit]) bar-length calc (calcs[0]). replaceFieldReferences rewrites every [Profit] instance on bind.

Categorical axis on cols — one floating bar per member, ordered by the computed-sort (DESC by SUM(Profit)); the running total accumulates left-to-right across these members. XML column-instance [none:Sub-Category:nk].

OPTIONAL P&L/waterfall row-type guard. Bind the row-type/category column when the data carries subtotal/total rows; rows whose value in this column is 'subtotal' or 'total' (case-insensitive intent; current XML categorical filters can express only explicit members) are EXCLUDED from the bridge so the running total counts each increment once. Use this for category/row-type fields like category, row_type, or anchor_category that tag summary rows separately from incremental line items.

calcs.notes: Template-owned GanttBar length: -SUM([Profit]) sizes each floating bar downward from its running-total row anchor so the bar spans this category's delta. A self-closing <calculation>, so parseTemplateCalcs sees it — a declared calc slot. Its one input is the bindable 'profit' slot; replaceFieldReferences rewrites [Profit] on bind.

hazards.detail: The rows axis [cum:sum:Profit:qk] is a <column-instance> of base [Profit] carrying a nested <table-calc type='CumTotal'> (Running Total). Because it is a column-INSTANCE with a nested table-calc (not a <column><calculation>), parseTemplateCalcs (self-closing <calculation> only) never sees it — it is NOT a declared calc slot and rides along verbatim on rows. Its base is the bindable 'profit' slot; replaceFieldReferences rewrites [Profit] inside the instance on bind. The running total is ORDER-DEPENDENT (accumulates across Sub-Category in the computed-sort order), so an unordered bound dimension yields an arbitrary cumulative path. Same class as ranking-bump-chart table-calc-rank-rides-along and slope-chart slope-filter-table-calc.

The source workbook carries a datasource-level <style-rule element='mark'> value->hue/glyph map (3 color / 4 shape maps, factory-detected) referencing Sub-Category and other workbook-shared fields. It is NOT carried into this template (no datasource_style sidecar) because it is irrelevant to this waterfall: the color that matters is the continuous SUM(Profit) encoding fully contained in the worksheet (<color column='[sum:Profit:qk]'>), NOT a categorical Sub-Category hue. Carrying it would also splice customer member values + federated datasource IDs into the repo (licensing/sensitive-data wall) — the same decision L1 made for spatial-choropleth-map. No fidelity loss for this template; on apply only the in-worksheet encoding is applied.

Product evidence (P&L waterfalls): real revenue/margin waterfalls carry MID-CHAIN SUBTOTAL members — e.g. 'Gross Profit' after the revenue/COGS block, then opex steps, then 'Operating Income'. This template treats EVERY member of the bound dimension as an independent step, adding its -SUM([Profit]) delta to the running total. A subtotal row is not an increment — it restates the running total to date — so if it is present as a member it DOUBLE-COUNTS: the cumulative path jumps by the subtotal's full value and every step after it is offset. The template cannot distinguish a subtotal member from a step member. Detection/guidance: bind a dimension of INCREMENTAL line items only (exclude subtotal/total members via a filter before binding), or model subtotals as reference lines / separate annotation rather than as steps. A tell-tale is a running total that ends at roughly 2x the true total, or a member whose delta equals the sum of the preceding steps.

Product evidence (product team, 2026-07) — has bitten someone: the running total only MEANS something when the bound category carries an EXPLICIT or SEMANTIC order (a P&L line-item sequence, a time order, or the computed sort this template applies: Sub-Category DESC by SUM(Profit)). Bind a nominal dimension with NO inherent order and let it fall back to ALPHABETICAL member order (e.g. product names A→Z) and the cumulative path becomes an arbitrary artifact of the labels — rename or re-sort the same rows and the bridge tells a different story. Bind an ordered/ordinal dimension or keep the measure sort; never read a cumulative build-up off an unordered alphabetical nominal. Tracked-hazard companion to the order-dependence avoid_when.

Product evidence (product team, 2026-07) — has bitten someone: when the source rows carry a MIXED currency_code (USD + EUR + GBP …), the bound measure must NOT be summed raw. The running total [cum:sum:Profit:qk] on rows and every -SUM([Profit]) bar delta add different currency units as one number, so the cumulative whole is silently WRONG (a data-correctness bug, not a formatting one). The template has no currency awareness. Convert to a single reporting currency, or filter to one currency_code, BEFORE binding a revenue/margin measure into the waterfall.

templates.description: A quota-attainment bullet chart for the product #1-frequency use case (sales/quota performance, UC1): one Bar per entity member (rep/region on rows) whose length is the ACTUAL measure on cols ([sum:Actual:qk]), with a per-cell QUOTA reference line (SUM([Quota]) drawn as refline0 on the SAME axis as the actual bar) so the gap between the bar and the line reads as attainment vs goal at a glance. Compiled hermetically over a CLEANED copy of the repo ranking-bullet-chart.xml exemplar (which had no manifest, excluded by an earlier lane as dead XML; the original dead file is left untouched): the Superstore-specific categorical filter and the Profit-Ratio color calc were dropped, and Sub-Category/Sales/Profit were generalized to Entity/Actual/Quota placeholders, so the slot vocabulary is exactly {entity, actual, quota} (optional categorical filter dims are NOT slots). Sibling of bullet-variance-chart (same actual-vs-target shape, quota-attainment intent). Live render-verified 2026-07-06 by the golden-parity gate (composite 98; see portability_evidence.render_evidence): structural 0.97297 (5/5 critical), pixel 1.0, binding-sanity sane via reconciled 17=17=EXPECTED_COUNT Copy>Data mark counts and caption-leak pass (the grafted Target Amount calc and the per-cell reference line both survived apply). Provenance: product team, 2026-07.
avoid_when: The actual and/or quota rows carry a MIXED currency_code (USD + EUR + GBP ...) that would be summed RAW: SUM([Actual]) on cols and the per-cell SUM([Quota]) reference line add different currency units as one number, so the attainment gap is silently wrong; convert to a single reporting currency (or filter to one currency_code) before binding a monetary measure.

There is no meaningful quota/goal/benchmark measure to compare against; without the reference line this collapses to a plain ranked bar (use ranking-ordered-bar), because a bullet's whole value is the actual-vs-quota gap.

slots.notes: The entity dimension on rows: one bullet row per member (rep / region / team). Optional categorical FILTER dims are NOT slots (the dead exemplar's Superstore Sub-Category filter was dropped on clean). XML column-instance [none:Entity:nk].
The ACTUAL/attained measure: the bar length on cols, SUM([Actual]) -> [sum:Actual:qk]. This is also the reference line's axis-column (the quota line sits on the same axis as the actual bar).

The QUOTA/target/goal measure that drives the reference line. Carried on the level-of-detail (lod) shelf as SUM([Quota]) -> [sum:Quota:qk] so it is in the view, and referenced by refline0 (value-column) as the per-cell quota line. replaceFieldReferences rewrites [Quota] in BOTH the lod instance and the reference-line on bind. See hazard quota-summed-per-cell / multi-currency-raw-sum.

hazards.detail: Product evidence (product team, 2026-07) - has bitten someone: when the actual and/or quota rows carry a MIXED currency_code (USD + EUR + GBP ...), neither measure may be summed raw. SUM([Actual]) on cols and the per-cell SUM([Quota]) reference line add different currency units as one number, so the attainment gap is silently WRONG (a data-correctness bug, not a formatting one). The template has no currency awareness. Convert to a single reporting currency, or filter to one currency_code, before binding a monetary measure.

The quota line is a per-cell reference line with formula='sum' (SUM([Quota])), so it aggregates the quota additively within each entity cell. If the bound quota is a rate/ratio, an average goal, or an already-rolled-up period total, SUM double-counts across the cell's rows and the line lands in the wrong place. Bind a quota that is additive at the entity grain, or switch the reference line's formula to average/median. (product team, 2026-07)

A mid-period ACTUAL compared against a FULL-period quota overstates the shortfall: the bar sits far below the quota line simply because the period is not over, not because the entity is behind. The template has no as-of/period-elapsed awareness: pro-rate the quota to the elapsed period, scale the actual to a run-rate, or label the as-of date before reading the gap as risk. (product team, 2026-07)

templates.description: A bump chart: rank (a table calc of an aggregated measure) plotted over a Year/Month date on cols, one Line per category (color), with a Circle dot and rank label at each period — shows how members' rankings rise and fall across time. Lane W11-F2: the source year pin was PARAMETERIZED (collapsed to level-members) so the date stays a data-driven Filters pill with no source literals.

slots.notes: Year part of the SAME bound date field (outer cols axis). Reuses Order Date at a second derivation — see hazard single-date-two-derivations.
Month part of the SAME bound date field (inner cols axis) — the period the rank is recomputed at.
One ranked line/color per member — the entities whose ranks bump over time.

The measure that is ranked. It is not on a shelf directly; it feeds the template-owned Rank(Sum([Sales])) table calc (see hazard table-calc-rank-rides-along). replaceFieldReferences rewrites [Sales] inside that formula on bind.

hazards.detail: The source workbook pinned Year of Order Date to member='2016' — a source-calendar literal that would render empty on a differently-shaped date. Lane F2's compile-time filter policy PARAMETERIZED it: the member pin was collapsed to the neutral all-members form (<groupfilter function='level-members' level='[yr:Order Date:ok]'/>), so Year stays a data-driven Filters-shelf pill (its <slices> entry preserved) with the source literal removed — the same neutral form the apply-path 3c neutralizer writes (idempotent).

The one [Order Date] field is used at BOTH Year and Month on cols. Against the single-date committed fixture the second temporal slot cannot bind a DISTINCT field, so fixture_bind=false — a valid, honest state (same as change-over-time-calendar-heatmap). On a real dataset both slots bind the same date field via qualified keys; the false reflects the fixture's one-date shape, not a template defect.

The Rank measure [Calculation_508343847073411072] is a table calc 'Rank(Sum([Sales]))' with a nested <table-calc>. Its <calculation> is NON-self-closing, so parseTemplateCalcs (self-closing only) does NOT see it — it is NOT a declared calc slot and rides along verbatim. Its one bindable input is Sales (the 'sales' slot); replaceFieldReferences rewrites [Sales] inside the formula on bind. The ranking assumes the bound measure is meaningfully rankable across the categories.

templates.description: A dot strip plot: one Circle mark per detail-grain member placed along an aggregated-measure axis, grouped by a category on rows, with a per-cell average reference line — a space-efficient way to rank/compare members within each row. slots.notes: Month of the bound date on the rows axis — one strip per month. Used at a single derivation (mn), so no qualified key is required.
Aggregated measure on the cols axis — the value each dot is placed at; also the per-cell average reference line.
Level-of-detail dimension — one Circle mark per member within each row's strip.

hazards.detail: The source workbook pinned Month of Order Date to a 1..6 (Jan–Jun) member list — a source-calendar window that would render empty/partial on a differently-shaped date. templates.description: Horizontal bars, one per category; bar length = measure; sorted descending by the measure.
avoid_when: The 'top' here is positional layering — marks drawn on top of a reference line or another chart layer — not a top-N ranking; a positional or annotation placement is not a sorted bar chart.

slots.notes: OPTIONAL small-multiples facet (W23-SM1): a spare categorical dimension the binder places on rows AHEAD of the ranked category pill, trellising the ranking into stacked panes (one ranked bar set per member). Optional → EXCLUDED from the required-bindable fixture_bind set (only {region, sales} count), so fast_path_eligible does not move. Binds ONLY when the ask names/implies a by-<dim> facet (small multiples / trellis / per <dim> / for each) AND a spare categorical remains after the required slots — it never steals the ranked (region) dim, and a bare 'by <dim>' never facets (fail-closed). XML declares [Facet] as a datasource-dependency column the field-swap injector renames to the bound dim; on-shelf pill placement for a bound facet is applied downstream (the default sorted-bar render is byte-unchanged). Modeled on box-plot-chart's required:false facet.

hazards.detail: computed-sort binds the dimension instance to the measure instance; both slots must bind or the sort dangles (both are required, so this is satisfied by coverage).

templates.description: A slope chart: a measure plotted against a truncated-quarter date, with a template-internal FIRST/LAST filter keeping only the two endpoint periods; one line per category shows the direction/magnitude of change between the two periods.

the measure whose endpoint values define each slope; also the value label.
truncated-quarter date on the columns axis; the FIRST/LAST filter reduces it to the two endpoint periods.

hazards.detail: A template-internal boolean table calc 'FIRST()==0 or LAST()==0' filters to the two endpoint periods. templates.description: Filled (choropleth) map: one Multipolygon per geographic region, shaded by a continuous color measure. Standard geo dimensions (Country + State) on level-of-detail with generated Latitude/Longitude on rows/cols; SUM(measure) on color as a custom-interpolated diverging gradient. Factory-compiled from the CookBook 'Choropleth Map Viz' worksheet; structurally compiled and render-verified (live-2026-07-06).
avoid_when: The ask is 'which region ranks highest/lowest' or needs precise cross-region value comparison — filled-area color is hard to compare quantitatively; prefer a ranked bar chart.
Regions differ wildly in physical size — large, low-value areas visually dominate (area is not magnitude); consider a symbol/bubble map sized by the measure instead.
The dimension has no geographic semantic-role (not a Country/State/etc.) — a choropleth needs fields Tableau can resolve to a filled geometry.

slots.notes: Geographic dimension on level-of-detail (outer region). Binds to a Country/Region-class field; XML column-instance [none:Country:nk].
Geographic dimension on level-of-detail (the shaded unit). Binds to a State/Province-class field; XML column-instance [none:State:nk].
Continuous color measure driving the diverging gradient (custom-interpolated). Binds to any measure; XML column-instance [sum:Profit:qk].

hazards.detail: The source workbook carries a datasource-level <style-rule element='mark'> with categorical value->hue / value->glyph maps for OTHER fields (State, Sub-Category, Region, Ship Mode, Segment, Measure Names, flag-icon shapes) shared across the workbook's sheets. It is NOT carried into this template (unlike the factory golden's datasource_style sidecar) because it is irrelevant to this choropleth — the color that matters is a continuous SUM(measure) diverging gradient fully contained in the worksheet XML (custom-interpolated color encoding). Carrying it would also splice customer member values and federated datasource IDs into the repo (licensing/sensitive-data wall). No fidelity loss for this template; on apply, only the in-worksheet gradient is applied.

The template plots generated [Latitude (generated)]/[Longitude (generated)] on rows/cols and a generated [Geometry (generated)] on the geometry encoding. These are Tableau-generated fields that exist only when the bound dimensions carry a geographic semantic-role — the binder must map the two geo slots to fields Tableau geocodes (e.g. Country/Region, State/Province), or the map will not render filled areas.

templates.description: A real latitude/longitude point symbol map: Longitude and Latitude measure columns are placed on Cols/Rows as AVG continuous axes, one Circle mark per detail dimension member, sized and colored by a measure. Use for datasets that already carry real coordinate columns rather than Tableau-generated geocoding fields.

slots.notes: Real longitude measure column, aggregated as AVG and placed on Columns as [avg:Longitude:qk].
Real latitude measure column, aggregated as AVG and placed on Rows as [avg:Latitude:qk].

Detail/grain dimension: one mark per member (e.g. City, Site, Team, or point name). Placed on the level-of-detail shelf as [none:Detail:nk]. Renamed from the draft's [Country] to a generic [Detail] because the XML declares no geographic semantic-role — calling it Country would imply geo behavior the template does not carry.

Measure used for BOTH symbol size and continuous color as [sum:Measure:qk]. REQUIRED (not optional): the XML hard-references [sum:Measure:qk] in the size encoding, the color encoding, and the datasource-level color style-rule; validateBinding SKIPS unbound optional slots, so leaving this optional would strand a dangling [Measure] template field on apply unless the target datasource happens to carry [Measure]. The current optional-slot apply glue is proven only for facets.

hazards.detail: UNKNOWN: current no-LLM quantitative binding is role-greedy, not latitude/longitude-name-affine; coordinate slots can swap unless a route/proposal test proves otherwise.
UNKNOWN: shipped map templates use generated Latitude/Longitude with mapsources; no committed real-measure lat/lon map precedent proves map layer behavior before live render/readback.
