templates.description: A line of an aggregated measure over a truncated date on the x-axis; shows trend across time.
avoid_when: The x-axis is a set of DISCRETE, non-temporal CATEGORIES (categorical members such as products or segments) instead of a continuous time axis. A line CONNECTS adjacent points and so IMPLIES CONTINUITY between them, drawing a misleading slope across unrelated categorical members; a cross-category breakdown with no time axis belongs on a bar chart. Keep this line template for a continuous time x-axis only — do not bind a discrete category to the x.

slots.notes: XML instance is [tmn:Order Date:qk] derivation='Month-Trunc'. 'tmn' is the real Month-Trunc short-form live Tableau writes; the manifest emits it verbatim (no normalization). templates.ts derivationMap keys both 'tmn' (canonical) and legacy 'tmo' to 'Month-Trunc'. temporal_from_string:true — a date-like STRING (e.g. a 'YYYY-MM' month) is accepted here; the apply path injects a DATEPARSE calc and truncates it to a continuous month axis (dateparseTemporalAxis.ts).

OPTIONAL small-multiples facet (W23-SM1): a spare categorical dimension the binder places on cols AHEAD of the truncated date pill, trellising the trend into side-by-side panes (one line per member). Optional → EXCLUDED from the required-bindable fixture_bind set (only {order_date, sales} count), so fast_path_eligible does not move. Binds ONLY when the ask names/implies a by-<dim> facet (small multiples / trellis / per <dim> / for each) AND a spare categorical remains after the required slots — a bare 'by <dim>' never facets (fail-closed), and it never steals a slot-bound dim. XML declares [Facet] as a datasource-dependency column the field-swap injector renames to the bound dim; on-shelf pill placement for a bound facet is applied downstream (the un-faceted default render is byte-unchanged). Modeled on box-plot-chart's required:false facet.

hazards.detail: RULING (H3.2 tmn/tmo reconciliation): 'tmn' is the REAL Month-Trunc short-form live Tableau writes — confirmed in this template XML (derivation='Month-Trunc' name='[tmn:Order Date:qk]'), in real Tableau Public workbooks (data/twb-example-index.json: [sqlserver…].[tmn:Order Date:qk], [dataengine…].[tmn:DateTime (Days):ok]), in src/metadata/fields.ts:434, and in tactics/tree/column-instance-prefixes.md. The binder/manifests standardize on canonical 'tmn'; templates.ts derivationMap accepts BOTH 'tmn' and legacy 'tmo' as aliases → 'Month-Trunc'. XML is ground truth; no derivation normalization is applied.

Product evidence (product team, 2026-07) — has bitten someone: a line mark connects adjacent points, so it IMPLIES CONTINUITY along the x-axis. The order_date slot must bind a real continuous time field; binding a DISCRETE category to the x draws a slope between unrelated members that reads as a trend where none exists (a 'compare X across categories' ask must route to a bar chart, not here). Tracked-hazard companion to the discrete-categories avoid_when: the classifier demotes an otherwise-clean line bind when the ask names a discrete categorical breakdown with no time axis.

templates.description: One floating (gantt) bar per category spanning a reference-line measure to a parsed actual score: a template SPLIT calc parses the combined actual score out of a compound '<code> <win>-<lose>' string (e.g. 'PHI 40-22' → 40+22=62), the size calc draws the span (actual − reference line), and an over/under SIGN calc colors each bar over (right/teal) vs under (left/red). Deviation of an actual outcome from a betting/reference line, per member.
avoid_when: the actual field is not a compound '<prefix> <winner>-<loser>' score string — the SPLIT parse assumes a single-token prefix, a space, then a hyphen-separated winner-loser pair (e.g. 'PHI 40-22'); a different shape parses the wrong combined score
both endpoints are already numeric measures — bind a simpler dual-measure or reference-line deviation chart instead of the string-parse calc

slots.notes: The row/category each floating bar is drawn for (one mark per member).

The reference-line/betting-line endpoint on columns AND the subtrahend of the size and over/under calcs. Derivation 'min' is authored fidelity from the checkpoint — correct only at one-row-per-category grain (see min-derivation-per-row hazard).

Compound-score STRING (e.g. 'PHI 40-22') SPLIT-parsed by the actual-score calc into a combined numeric score (40+22=62). Bindable + required so templates.ts rewriteFormulaFieldRefs repairs the bare [Actual Input] token and Gate 6 can prove the dependency is bound. Never placed on a shelf (no column-instance).

calcs.formula: INT( SPLIT( TRIM( SPLIT( [Actual Input], "-", 1 ) ), " ", 2 ) ) + INT( TRIM( SPLIT( [Actual Input], "-", 2 ) ) )
calcs.notes: GOLDEN actual-score parse (Over/Under and Betting Lines golden.twb: Winning Score = INT(SPLIT(TRIM(SPLIT([Final],'-',1)),' ',2)), Losing Score = INT(TRIM(SPLIT([Final],'-',2))), Total = Winning + Losing). Replaces the checkpoint's NULL-producing bare INT([Actual Input]) — INT() of a leading-alpha compound string ('PHI 40-22') is NULL, collapsing every bar to zero span. Not shelved; consumed as a template-internal input by the size and over/under calcs.
avoid_when: the actual string is not '<prefix> <winner>-<loser>' — the nested SPLIT/TRIM assumes a single-token prefix before the space and a hyphen between winner and loser scores

calcs.notes: Bar length = parsed actual score − reference line. Consumes the template-internal actual-score calc (SPLIT-parsed) and the bindable Reference Value; rewriteFormulaFieldRefs repairs [Reference Value] on bind, and leaves the template-owned [Calculation_ActualScore] untouched (unmapped).
avoid_when: there is more than one row per category — the 'min' derivation collapses multi-row members to their minimum span

calcs.notes: GOLDEN over/under color = sign(actual − line): +1 over (right/teal #76b7b2), -1 under (left/red #e15759), 0 push. Replaces the checkpoint's mis-chosen raw O/U Winner (Favorite/Underdog spread result) color. Shelved discretely as [sum:Calculation_OverUnder:ok] with a mark palette style-rule so the color splits by true over/under, not the spread.
avoid_when: over/under is not defined against the reference line — this SIGN colors purely by (parsed actual − reference), so a reference that is not the true betting/expected line mislabels the split

hazards.detail: The actual-score calc parses a combined score out of a compound '<prefix> <winner>-<loser>' string via nested SPLIT/TRIM (GOLDEN semantics). The parse is dataset-shape-specific: a different string shape (multi-token prefix, no hyphen, non-numeric scores) yields NULL/garbage. Advisory only — surfaced via avoid_when and gated by render_verified, not a hard fast_path_blocker.

The calcs reference bare bindable [Actual Input]/[Reference Value]; templates.ts rewriteFormulaFieldRefs repairs them ONLY because both are bindable bare-key slots. The template-internal [Calculation_ActualScore] ref is intentionally left unmapped (a template-owned calc). Gate 6 asserts the bindable deps are bound before injection.

The size calc and the reference line use derivation 'min' (authored fidelity from the checkpoint). Min equals the row value only when there is exactly one mark per Category; a category with multiple rows collapses to the minimum. Bind at one-row-per-category grain or override the derivation.

templates.description: Dual-mark arrow chart (Line shaft + Shape arrowhead) comparing an actual combined score to a betting/reference line per member, newest-first, with text label columns (season / id / line) and an inline O/U-diff spine. Arrows point right/teal when the actual finished OVER the line and left/red when UNDER, colored by a SIGN(actual - line) calc. Faithful compile of the Super Bowl 'O/U Line' golden worksheet.
avoid_when: the actual field is not a compound '<prefix> <winner>-<loser>' score string — the nested SPLIT/TRIM parse assumes a single-token prefix, a space, then a hyphen-separated winner-loser pair (e.g. 'PHI 40-22'); a different shape parses the wrong combined score
the row identifier is not a '<name> (<year>)' pattern — the season/id split parses the parenthetical year out of the member label

distinct teal/red over/under hue and the left/right arrowhead glyphs are required on first render — that palette+shape mapping is datasource-level style in the golden and is NOT carried by this worksheet-only template (see hazards datasource-style-dropped)

slots.notes: The compound member label '<name> (<year>)' (e.g. 'Super Bowl LIX (2024)'). SPLIT-parsed by the season and id calcs into the row header text columns. Never shelved raw.
Compound-score STRING (e.g. 'KC 25-22') SPLIT-parsed into winning + losing scores that sum to the actual combined score. Never shelved raw.

The O/U betting line. Shown as a discrete text label column on rows ([sum:Total Score Bet:ok]), as an endpoint of the arrow's shared measure axis ([sum:Total Score Bet:qk]), and as the subtrahend of the over/under sign and O/U-diff calcs.

calcs.notes: TRIM(SPLIT([Super Bowl],'(',1)) — the member name/id before the parenthetical year; feeds the SB # roman-numeral label calc.
int(TRIM(SPLIT(SPLIT([Super Bowl],'(',2),')',1)))-1 — the season year parsed from the parenthetical. The outermost rows dimension and the DESC (newest-first) sort key.

hazards.detail: CARRIED (fidelity fix): the golden's datasource-level <style-rule element='mark'> value→hue (color palette) + value→glyph (shape) maps (2 color + 2 shape) AND the 1 datasource-scope column-instance declaration(s) they require are captured into manifest.datasource_style and re-spliced on apply (evals/lib/tier1-fastpath.mjs spliceDatasourceStyle) at the proven anchors — column-instance before <layout>, <style> before <semantic-values> — with calc refs namespaced per-apply identically to the worksheet fragment. Previously dropped by extractWorksheet (worksheet-only). Original loss detail: The golden's discrete over/under color palette (teal #76b7b2 over, red #e15759 under) AND the arrowhead shape mapping (:filled/right-triangle over, :filled/left-triangle under) are declared in the DATASOURCE-level <style><style-rule element='mark'> block (golden twb lines 441-460), NOT inside the <worksheet>. scripts/compile-checkpoint-template.mjs extractWorksheet extracts only the <worksheet> element, so this value->hue / value->glyph map is dropped at compile time. The template carries the color/shape ENCODINGS (which field drives them) but the marks fall back to the default palette + default shape on apply. Faithful hue+glyph requires re-injecting the datasource-level mark style; not expressible in the worksheet-only slot-spec.

The actual-score and season/id calcs parse combined tokens out of compound strings via nested SPLIT/TRIM (golden semantics): 'Final' as '<prefix> <winner>-<loser>' and 'Super Bowl' as '<name> (<year>)'. Dataset-shape-specific — a different string shape yields NULL/garbage. Advisory (surfaced via avoid_when + render_verified gate), not a hard fast_path_blocker.
